data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so files created during the build are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs` before `CoreCompile`, but it never adds the file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs are evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up by the implicit `**/*.cs` glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it in `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since this is exactly the scenario it covers.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** — before any targets execute. So the file generated by `GenerateSampleCode` exists on disk but isn't in the `Compile` item list when the compiler runs.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `<Compile>` (so it's compiled) and `<FileWrites>` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
